purpose: Define the architecture of scripts/validate_harness.py so harness checks are separated into explicit layers instead of an accident-prone linear pile of regressions.
authority_note: This file owns validator layer boundaries, section ownership, and side-effect policy. It does not own product truth, UI design truth, workspace classification, or PR delivery policy; those remain in their owner specs.

product_truth.none: Harness architecture is repo governance truth, not product truth. It protects product truth by making validator responsibilities explicit.

layers.name: Bootstrap shared runtime
layers.responsibility: Create the shared validation environment and load active specs.

layers.name: Truth and spec consistency
layers.responsibility: Check active truth, owner/mirror consistency, product contract mirrors, and visual-language mirrors using local files only.

layers.name: Workspace hygiene and context boundary
layers.responsibility: Check active workspace classification and default agent read boundaries without inspecting generated dependency trees.

layers.name: Delivery governance and PR gates
layers.responsibility: Check branch policy, hooks, branch protection, workflow wiring, PR body gates, and local delivery validators.

layers.name: Design governance and design-search regressions
layers.responsibility: Check design artifact lifecycle, quarantine, single-card UX contract, design-search fixtures, and visual-output PR gate regressions.

layers.name: Runtime smoke and app/backend behavior
layers.responsibility: Runtime behavior is verified by dedicated CI jobs, not by the pure harness layers. validate_harness.py may check that those jobs are wired, but it must not become the runtime smoke runner.

anti_patterns.name: put_remote_or_runtime_side_effects_in_truth_or_workspace_layers
anti_patterns.correction: keep_truth_spec_and_workspace_hygiene_layers_pure_and_move side effects into delivery governance or CI runtime smoke jobs
